definition_population.content: Corresponds to a recently described monogenic disease from a germline gain-of-function PLCG1 variant, described as a new immune dysregulation disorder; related to but distinct from PLCG2-associated PLAID/APLAID.

sources.quote: A gain-of-function variation in PLCG1 causes a new immune dysregulation disease.

epidemiology.content: No population prevalence/incidence figure found; described from a single new case/family report, consistent with ultra-rare status.

sources.quote: PLCG2-associated immune dysregulation (PLAID) comprises broad and distinct clinical presentations related to functional classes of genetic variants.

biology.content: PLCG1 regulates NF-kB, ERK, MAPK, and NFAT signaling; germline gain-of-function PLCG1 mutation was first reported causal for this disease in 2023; somatic PLCG1 mutations also recur in T-cell lymphomas, underscoring its role in T-cell activation/proliferation.

sources.quote: Phospholipase C (PLC) gamma1 is a critical enzyme regulating nuclear factor-kB (NF-kB), extracellular signal-related kinase, mitogen-activated protein kinase, and nuclear factor of activated T cells signaling pathways, yet germline PLCG1 mutation in human disease has not been reported.

treatment.content: No FDA-approved disease-specific therapy identified in this pass.

regulatory_trials.content: ClinicalTrials.gov search on broader term 'immune dysregulation' returned 65 total trials; gene-specific 'PLCG1' search returned zero trials.
